abstract.text: A 3D vector using integer coordinates.

inlineContent.text: A 3-element structure that can be used to represent 3D grid coordinates or any other triplet of integers.
It uses integer coordinates and is therefore preferable to
when exact precision is required. Note that the values are limited to 32 bits, and unlike
this cannot be configured with an engine build option. Use integer or
In a boolean context, a Vector3i will evaluate to
. Otherwise, a Vector3i will always evaluate to

abstract.text: Back unit vector. Represents the local direction of back, and the global direction of south.
Types that conform to VariantStorable can be stored in a Variant and can be extracted
back out of a Variant.
Forward unit vector. Represents the local direction of forward, and the global direction of north.
The vector’s X component. Also accessible by using the index position
Returns the squared length (squared magnitude) of this vector.

by the given integer.
Returns a new vector with all components in absolute values (i.e. positive).

by the given integer.
Framework to write Godot Game Extensions using the Swift Programming Language.
Up unit vector.
Compares two
vectors by first checking if the X value of the left vector is greater than the X value of the
vector. If the X values are exactly equal, then it repeats this check with the Y values of the two vectors, and then with the Z values. This operator is useful for sorting vectors.
A 3D vector using floating point coordinates.
Types that conform to VariantRepresentable can be stored directly in
with no conversion.  These include all of the Variant types from Godot (for
example
), Godot objects (those that subclass
SwiftGodot.Object) as well as the built-in Swift types UInt8, Int64 and Double.
Structs and scalar types use their own layout for storage.

Left unit vector. Represents the local direction of left, and the global direction of west.
